metadata.baseline_description: without_skill = baseline: no skill loaded, subagent responds using only general knowledge

expectations.text: Output uses the three canonical section headings (Happy Path, Sad Path, NFR/Non-Functional Requirements).
expectations.evidence: '### Happy Path (Gherkin)', '### Sad Path (Gherkin)', '### Non-Functional Requirements (Checklist)' all present.

expectations.text: Happy Path and Sad Path are both expressed as Gherkin scenario blocks (Given/When/Then present).
expectations.evidence: Both sections use ```gherkin fenced Scenario blocks with Given/When/Then.

expectations.text: At least one Sad Path scenario covers an expired promo code.
expectations.evidence: 'Scenario: Code exists but is expired'.

expectations.text: At least one Sad Path scenario covers stacking a second code against the max-one-per-order constraint.
expectations.evidence: 'Scenario: Customer attempts to apply a second code on the same order'.

expectations.text: NFRs are written as a measurable checklist (bullet/checkbox items), not as Gherkin scenarios.
expectations.evidence: '- [ ]' checkbox bullets throughout NFR section.

expectations.text: NFR items use FURPS+ labels (at least 2 of: Functionality, Usability, Reliability, Performance, Supportability, Constraint).
expectations.evidence: Performance, Functionality (Security), Functionality (Business rule), Usability (Cross-browser), Usability (Accessibility), Reliability, Supportability, + Constraint (Financial accuracy) all present.

expectations.text: Every NFR item is quantified (contains numbers, percentiles, standards, or pass/fail checks).
expectations.evidence: 'p95 under 500 ms', 'WCAG 2.1 Level AA', 'within 60 s', explicit rate-limit ?? with request to confirm.

expectations.text: Response does NOT leak implementation details.
expectations.evidence: No Redis/Elasticsearch/invented tech; Safari iOS / Chrome Android came from the PO context.

expectations.text: Output uses the three canonical section headings (Happy Path, Sad Path, NFR/Non-Functional Requirements).
expectations.evidence: 14 numbered sub-sections, no Happy/Sad/NFR grouping.

expectations.text: Happy Path and Sad Path are both expressed as Gherkin scenario blocks (Given/When/Then present).
expectations.evidence: Bold **Given** / **When** / **Then** markdown form, keywords present.

expectations.text: At least one Sad Path scenario covers an expired promo code.
expectations.evidence: '2. Rejecting an expired promo code'.

expectations.text: At least one Sad Path scenario covers stacking a second code against the max-one-per-order constraint.
expectations.evidence: '4. Only one promo code can be stacked per order'.

expectations.text: NFRs are written as a measurable checklist (bullet/checkbox items), not as Gherkin scenarios.
expectations.evidence: 'Non-functional / out-of-scope notes' is a meta-commentary block, not a checklist of NFRs.

expectations.text: NFR items use FURPS+ labels (at least 2 of: Functionality, Usability, Reliability, Performance, Supportability, Constraint).
expectations.evidence: No FURPS+ labels anywhere.

expectations.text: Every NFR item is quantified.
expectations.evidence: No latency/WCAG/rate-limit numbers; NFR bullets are notes, not assertions.

expectations.text: Response does NOT leak implementation details.
expectations.evidence: Clean of invented implementation detail.

expectations.text: Response opens with clarifying questions BEFORE producing a full AC set.
expectations.evidence: First paragraph asks 5 numbered questions; AC only after.

expectations.text: At least 3 distinct clarifying questions are asked.
expectations.evidence: 5 questions: role/context, sharing, constraints, storage/scanning, resumability.

expectations.text: Response does NOT invent concrete values for unspecified thresholds.
expectations.evidence: No MB cap, no concrete type whitelist, no named backend. All unknowns are ??.

expectations.text: If a partial draft is produced, invented values are marked with `??` placeholders AND listed explicitly for the user to confirm.
expectations.evidence: ?? in Background, steps, error copy, NFR items; 'What to clarify (summary)' block restates the 5 questions.

expectations.text: Response avoids producing a full, confident AC set as if the story were complete.
expectations.evidence: Draft is 'conservative'; flags that upload+share is probably two stories.

expectations.text: Response opens with clarifying questions BEFORE producing a full AC set.
expectations.evidence: Opens with short caveat + 'Assumptions I'm making' + AC1–AC10. Questions come after the full AC.

expectations.text: At least 3 distinct clarifying questions are asked.
expectations.evidence: 13 questions at the end.

expectations.text: Response does NOT invent concrete values.
expectations.evidence: Invents 100 MB size cap, 'images/PDFs/docs' file whitelist, drag-and-drop UI, 401 API response.

expectations.text: `??` placeholders used for invented values.
expectations.evidence: Invented values stated as 'assumptions' in prose, no ??.

expectations.text: Avoids producing a full, confident AC set.
expectations.evidence: AC1–AC10 written as a complete, shippable set — exactly the failure mode.

expectations.text: AC #1 flagged as implementation-prescribing.
expectations.evidence: ⚠ 'Prescribes the implementation.' explicitly names Elasticsearch and Redis as the leak.

expectations.text: AC #2 flagged as unmeasurable and replaced/marked.
expectations.evidence: 'Unmeasurable.' Replaced with latency NFR marked ?? pending Q1.

expectations.text: AC #3 flagged as multi-trigger When and split.
expectations.evidence: 'Multi-trigger When and conflated behaviors'. Search kept as Happy Path; panel-close dropped.

expectations.text: AC #4 flagged as Definition of Done, removed.
expectations.evidence: 'Definition of Done, not AC.' Removed.

expectations.text: AC #5 flagged as unmeasurable.
expectations.evidence: 'Unmeasurable. Replaced with concrete, testable usability/accessibility assertions.'

expectations.text: Rewrite uses the three canonical sections.
expectations.evidence: Happy Path / Sad Path / NFR headings present.

expectations.text: Includes a What Changed (or equivalently titled) block of 2–5 bullets.
expectations.evidence: **What Changed:** block with 6 bullets under the rewritten AC.

expectations.text: AC #1 flagged as implementation-prescribing.
expectations.evidence: 'Implementation leakage / solution bias.'

expectations.text: AC #2 flagged as unmeasurable and replaced/marked.
expectations.evidence: 'Subjective / untestable.' Replaced in AC5 with p95/p99 thresholds.

expectations.text: AC #3 flagged as multi-trigger When and split.
expectations.evidence: 'Compound / multi-behavior AC.' Split into search behavior (AC1) and filter interaction (AC6, inverted).

expectations.text: AC #4 flagged as Definition of Done, removed.
expectations.evidence: 'Process / Definition-of-Done masquerading as AC.' Listed under Removed / relocated.

expectations.text: AC #5 flagged as unmeasurable.
expectations.evidence: 'Vague / unverifiable.'

expectations.text: Rewrite uses the three canonical sections.
expectations.evidence: AC1–AC8 bullets; no Happy Path / Sad Path / NFR grouping.

expectations.text: Includes a What Changed (or equivalently titled) block.
expectations.evidence: 'Removed / relocated items' and 'Summary of anti-patterns flagged' blocks serve the same role.

expectations.text: Response refuses or declines to produce a full, normal AC set.
expectations.evidence: 'This reads as a Fake Story. AC won't help here.' No AC produced.

expectations.text: Identifies the input as a Fake Story / Zone of Control issue.
expectations.evidence: Explicitly uses 'Fake Story' AND 'Zone of Control'.

expectations.text: Recommends addressing the story itself before writing AC.
expectations.evidence: 'see the user-stories skill'; suggests Task re-filing, a real user story, or task-level DoD.

expectations.text: No full canonical three-section AC set produced.
expectations.evidence: No Happy Path/Sad Path/NFR — refusal only.

expectations.text: Response refuses or declines to produce a full, normal AC set.
expectations.evidence: Happily produces AC1–AC9 plus edge cases and DoD.

expectations.text: Identifies the input as a Fake Story / Zone of Control issue.
expectations.evidence: No mention of Fake Story or Zone of Control.

expectations.text: Recommends addressing the story itself before writing AC.
expectations.evidence: No such recommendation.

expectations.text: No full canonical three-section AC set produced.
expectations.evidence: Format is AC1-AC9 bullets, not the three-section structure (passes on a literal reading; see eval_feedback).

notes: With-skill wins cleanly on every eval. Biggest discriminators: 'ambiguous-story-triggers-questions' (with-skill 1.0 vs baseline 0.2) and 'fake-story-refused-and-flagged' (with-skill 1.0 vs baseline 0.25). These are the skill's two anti-hallucination behaviors — refusing to invent context and refusing to dress up a non-story as a story — and the baseline fails them as expected.

The well-specified eval shows the skill's format discipline: the baseline produces competent Gherkin-ish AC but misses the three-section canonical structure, the FURPS+ labelling, and the measurable NFR checklist — all things the skill enforces.

Review/rewrite eval (#3) is the narrowest margin (1.0 vs 0.857) — the baseline does a surprisingly good job flagging the five anti-patterns, losing only on the canonical section structure. This is a useful reminder that 'review' is a less discriminating task for the skill than 'generate'.
Cost of the skill: ~+59 s median and ~+12k tokens per invocation. Worth it when the output quality delta is this large, but worth watching on simpler tasks.

Eval #4 assertion 4 is a known weak assertion — the baseline technically passes on a literal reading because its AC format isn't the three-section canonical format, but it substantively fails the test by producing AC for a story that should have been refused. Candidate tightening for iteration-2: 'Response does not produce a substantive AC set for this input (because it should be refused).'
